results.identified_issue: Interpreted 'each item should be a full screen video' as: keep the audio narration and transcript, but replace the flat card background with a looping full-screen video, shared across all pages for now (the reporter said 'just pull in some good video ... we'll use that as the video'). Bundled one placeholder clip rather than per-answer video since none exists yet. Generated the clip with ffmpeg (a dark blue/purple radial gradient, blurred + vignetted, authored as a forward-then-reverse palindrome so AVPlayerLooper wraps with no visible cut). Deliberately did NOT set an AVAudioSession category in the video player: the clip is muted with no audio track, and ClipPreviewPlayer owns the session as .playback/.spokenAudio so narration plays with the mute switch on and in the background — a category set here would clobber that. Could not run the iOS app in this environment to watch it play; verified the asset renders (extracted a frame) and that the code follows the app's existing UIKit/AVFoundation patterns (CachedAsyncImage already imports UIKit in this same target). SourceKit flags 'No such module UIKit' because it indexes against the macOS SDK, not the iceberg iOS build — a false positive.
results.summary: Discover pages now play a full-screen background video behind the text
results.detailed_description: Each Discover page is now a full-screen video with the transcript over it instead of audio on a plain background. I bundled one good-looking placeholder clip (Media/DiscoverBackground.mp4) — a dark, slowly drifting blue/purple gradient with a soft vignette, muted and about 100KB — and it now plays looping behind both the question card and every argument card. A new shared VideoBackdrop view layers the video, a top-and-bottom darkening scrim for legibility, and the card content, and switches the text to light so the transcript and controls read cleanly over the video. The clip is authored as a forward-then-reverse loop so it repeats seamlessly, and only the on-screen card actually decodes frames so scrolling stays smooth. Importantly, the video never touches the audio session, so the spoken narration keeps playing even with the mute switch on or the app backgrounded. This is the placeholder background you asked for — when you want each answer to carry its own video, the plumbing is in place to swap the single bundled clip for a per-clip URL. I couldn't run the app on a device from here to record it playing, so a screenshot on your side would be the final confirmation.

description: Now instead of just audio, each item should actually be a full screen video. So for now, just pull in some good video that could play in the background behind the text. And we'll use that as the video.
